mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 150k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts (HOUST) and GDP growth (GDPC1) signal weakening demand conditions that trigger risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with HOUST < 1000 and GDPC1 < 0.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation exceeding 2.5% over 3 months signals central bank inaction on price pressures, leading to real rate declines and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPI > 2.5% periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 5%+ decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals manufacturing weakness that reduces corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO < 105 periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp+ rise in unemployment (UNRATE) during high wage growth periods signals labor market overheating, prompting rate hikes that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in interaction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit (RSAFS) alongside declining housing starts (HOUST) signals overleveraged households and weak demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in interaction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A 3%+ quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) combined with stable industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong economic growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in interaction periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.20.
